title: The Darkness Before Them
author: Matthew Ward
prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is verbatim from the "The Darkness Before Them" book by Matthew Ward?
Options:
A. Her eyes lit up. “You see? I knew you had missed me. This is Tanith,” she said breezily, indicating the slender younger woman standing farther back in the doorway. Clad in belted white robes, hands gloved to the elbows, her pale, rose-tinted cheeks framed by tight blonde curls, she looked as if she wanted nothing more than to dissolve into the stone archway. “Tanith, this is Castellan Damant. He handles everything around here. If he likes you, he might permit you to use his first name. He sometimes forgets his station, but don’t hold it against him.”
B. Her eyes shone brightly. "You see? I was sure you had missed me. This is Tanith," she said airily, motioning to the slim younger woman lingering deeper in the doorway. Dressed in ivory belted robes, hands gloved to the elbows and pale, pink-flushed cheeks surrounded by tight blonde ringlets, she appeared as though she wished for nothing more than to fade away into the stone of the arch. "Tanith, this is Castellan Damant. He takes care of everything around here. If he's fond of you, he may allow you to call him by his first name. He tends to forget his position, but you shouldn't judge him for it."  
C. Her eyes lit up. "See? I knew you had missed me. This is Tanith," she said, gesturing to the slender younger woman standing farther back in the doorway. Wearing belted white robes with elbow-length gloves, her pale cheeks framed by tight blonde curls, she looked like she wanted to vanish into the stone archway. "Tanith, meet Castellan Damant. He manages everything around here. If he likes you, he might let you use his first name. He sometimes forgets his role, but don't hold it against him."
D. Her eyes gleamed. “You see? I  knew  you’d missed me. This is Tanith.” The airy wave of her hand encompassed a younger, slender woman standing deeper in the doorway. Garbed in belted ivory robes, her hands gloved to the elbow and her pale, rose-blushed cheeks framed by tight blonde ringlets, she looked for all the world as though she wanted nothing more than to bleed away into the stone of the entryway’s arch. “Tanith, this is Castellan Damant. He takes care of, well,  everything  around here. If he likes you, he might let you call him by his first name. He has a tendency to forget his place, but you shouldn’t hold that against him.” 
Answer:
